skill.name: flow-nexus-platform
skill.description: Guides Flow Nexus platform operations for authentication, sandboxes, deployments, payments, storage, challenges, and monitoring.
skill.summary: Manage Flow Nexus accounts, sandboxes, deployments, payments, storage, challenges, and execution monitoring.
skill.author: Flow Nexus

security_audit.summary: Static execution findings are mostly false positives because SKILL.md contains fenced JavaScript MCP examples, not Ruby or shell backtick execution. Confirmed risk remains medium because the documented workflows can run remote sandbox code, install packages, pass secrets, deploy applications, manage storage, and configure payment auto-refill. No evidence found of malicious exfiltration intent or prompt injection text.

medium_findings.title: Remote Sandbox Code Execution
medium_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE: The skill documents sandbox creation, package installation, startup scripts, and code execution through Flow Nexus MCP tools. This is legitimate platform functionality, but it can execute arbitrary user-provided code in a remote environment and should require user confirmation.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced examples explicitly create sandboxes, install packages, provide startup scripts, and execute code. The behavior is documented as intended functionality, so the risk is real but not evidence of malicious intent.

medium_findings.title: Credential and Secret Handling in Platform Examples
medium_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE: Several examples pass API keys, database URLs, NEXTAUTH_SECRET, and an Anthropic key into sandbox or deployment configuration. This is expected for deployments, but it creates exposure risk if users paste real secrets into shared prompts, logs, or untrusted sandboxes.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The lines include credential-like field names and connection strings in environment variables and deployment variables. The examples use placeholders, which reduces severity but does not remove the operational risk.

medium_findings.title: Financial Actions Through Payment and Auto-Refill Tools
medium_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE: The skill documents creating payment links and enabling auto-refill for credits. This can affect billing or account balances when connected to a live Flow Nexus account, so agents should require explicit approval before use.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The examples explicitly create a payment link and configure auto-refill with dollar amounts. The impact depends on live account state, so medium severity is appropriate.

low_findings.title: Network and Storage Operations Require Scope Control
low_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE: The skill includes examples for fetch calls, support URLs, storage upload, public URL creation, real-time subscriptions, and generated file retrieval. These are normal platform features but can expose data if users choose public buckets or broad subscriptions.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The network and storage behavior is clearly present, but the examples are generic and do not show exfiltration. The risk is mainly misuse or overbroad access.

low_findings.title: Static External Command and Weak Crypto Alerts Are Mostly False Positives
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The many Ruby backtick execution findings are caused by Markdown code fences and JavaScript template literals in documentation. The weak cryptographic algorithm alerts hit descriptive text such as JWT, OAuth2, versioning, challenge categories, and assistant messages, not actual weak crypto implementation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review shows Markdown examples and plain descriptive text, not executable Ruby backticks or crypto code. The surrounding context strongly supports a false-positive classification.

low_findings.title: Reconnaissance Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The system reconnaissance and network reconnaissance alerts do not correspond to scanning behavior. The referenced areas describe credit earning, challenge troubleshooting, and public support resources.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced context contains marketplace credit guidance, troubleshooting text, and support links. I did not find evidence of host, port, or network scanning instructions.

dangerous_patterns.title: Sandbox Execution With User-Supplied Code
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill demonstrates sending code strings to mcp__flow-nexus__sandbox_execute. This pattern is powerful and should be gated by trust boundaries, timeouts, and explicit user approval.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The examples directly invoke a sandbox execution API with code content. The capability is intentional but potentially dangerous.

dangerous_patterns.title: Package Installation and Startup Scripts
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill shows installing packages, running commands, and using startup scripts in sandboxes. These operations can execute dependency install scripts or arbitrary setup commands.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The documented fields include install_packages, run_commands, and startup_script. These are normal devops controls, but they materially expand execution risk.

dangerous_patterns.title: Secret-Like Environment Values
dangerous_patterns.description: The examples include API keys, database URLs, NEXTAUTH_SECRET, and Anthropic key fields. Users should pass only scoped secrets and avoid exposing real values in prompts or logs.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The field names are credential-like and are embedded in deployment or sandbox configuration examples. The examples are placeholders, so the concern is exposure during real use.

dangerous_patterns.title: Billing Automation
dangerous_patterns.description: The payment examples can create payment links and enable automatic refill. Any agent using these tools should request explicit user approval before changing billing settings.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The documented calls include amount values and auto-refill thresholds. The operations are account-dependent but could produce financial impact.

content.value_statement: Flow Nexus users need one place to coordinate accounts, sandboxes, deployments, billing, storage, and challenges. This skill provides practical MCP workflows for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code users.
actual_capabilities: Guides user registration, login, authentication status, password reset, email verification, profile updates, and user statistics.
Documents sandbox creation, configuration, package installation, code execution, logs, file upload, status checks, and cleanup.
Covers app search, template browsing, app publishing, app updates, template deployment, market data, and analytics.
Explains credit balances, transaction history, payment links, auto-refill settings, subscription tiers, and earning credits.
Shows challenge browsing, solution submission, challenge completion, leaderboards, achievements, and reward categories.
Includes storage uploads, file listing, signed URLs, real-time subscriptions, execution streams, and generated file retrieval.
limitations: Requires Flow Nexus MCP tools, a Flow Nexus account, and valid authentication before workflows can run.
Examples use placeholder values and must be adapted before use with real projects or credentials.
Billing, auto-refill, deployment, and sandbox execution can affect live accounts and should require explicit approval.
Does not independently audit deployed applications, package dependencies, challenge solutions, or stored data for security issues.

use_cases.title: Launch a Development Sandbox
use_cases.description: Create a Node, Python, React, Next.js, or Claude Code sandbox with packages, environment variables, startup commands, logs, and cleanup steps.

use_cases.title: Publish and Deploy Templates
use_cases.description: Search templates, publish applications, deploy backend services, update metadata, review analytics, and manage installed apps from one workflow.

use_cases.title: Manage Credits and Challenges
use_cases.description: Check balances, configure credit purchases, submit coding challenge solutions, track achievements, and review leaderboards.

prompt_templates.title: Check My Flow Nexus Account
prompt_templates.prompt: Check my Flow Nexus authentication status, summarize my profile, and tell me what account setup steps are still missing.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Safe Sandbox
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a Flow Nexus development sandbox for a Node API. Ask before using real secrets, install only required packages, and set a one-hour timeout.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare an App Deployment
prompt_templates.prompt: Review available backend templates, recommend one for my service, prepare deployment variables, and explain each approval needed before deployment.

prompt_templates.title: Operate a Full Platform Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Plan a Flow Nexus workflow that creates a sandbox, tests a challenge solution, publishes a template, checks credits, and streams execution status.

output_examples.input: User asks to create a sandbox for an Express API.
output: Confirms the template, package list, timeout, and whether secrets are needed.
Creates the sandbox only after approval and reports the sandbox identifier and status.
Suggests cleanup steps to stop or delete the sandbox when work is complete.

output_examples.input: User asks to deploy a backend template.
output: Lists matching templates and highlights the safest option for the requested use case.
Summarizes required variables and warns before using database URLs or API keys.
Requests approval before deployment and then reports deployment status and next checks.

output_examples.input: User asks to review account credits and auto-refill.
output: Shows the current balance and recent payment history in plain language.
Explains the auto-refill threshold and amount before making changes.
Requests explicit approval before creating payment links or changing billing settings.

best_practices: Ask for explicit approval before sandbox execution, deployments, payment links, auto-refill, or public storage URL creation.
Use scoped placeholder values in prompts, then inject real secrets only through approved Flow Nexus environment controls.
Clean up unused sandboxes, temporary files, subscriptions, and execution streams to reduce cost and data exposure.
anti_patterns: Do not paste production API keys, database passwords, or payment details into shared prompts or chat history.
Do not enable auto-refill, publish paid templates, or deploy apps without confirming account and billing impact.
Do not store sensitive files in public buckets or create broad real-time subscriptions without a clear access scope.

faq.question: What does this skill help manage?
faq.answer: It helps manage Flow Nexus authentication, sandboxes, deployments, payments, credits, challenges, storage, and monitoring workflows.

faq.question: Does it run without Flow Nexus MCP tools?
faq.answer: No. The workflows require the Flow Nexus MCP tools and an authenticated Flow Nexus account.

faq.question: Can it execute code?
faq.answer: Yes. It documents remote sandbox execution through Flow Nexus tools, so users should approve code execution before it runs.

faq.question: Is it safe to use real secrets?
faq.answer: Use only scoped secrets and avoid placing real values in prompts. Prefer approved environment variable controls.

faq.question: Can it affect billing?
faq.answer: Yes. Payment links and auto-refill settings can affect billing when used with a live account.

faq.question: Does it audit deployed applications?
faq.answer: No. It documents deployment workflows but does not verify the security of application code or dependencies.
